parts.description: The tooltip popup. Renders the content with `role="tooltip"`.

Structural directive that teleports the tooltip content into a container (default `document.body`)
while the tooltip is open, and keeps it mounted until any CSS exit `@keyframes` finishes.

Apply it with the `*` microsyntax on the positioner — `<div *rdxTooltipPortal rdxTooltipPositioner>`
— or as an explicit `<ng-template rdxTooltipPortal>`. For a custom container use the explicit form
with `[container]`.

Dev-mode guard: `rdxTooltipPortal` used to be an attribute directive on a `<div>`. It is now
structural, so the old `<div rdxTooltipPortal>` markup would silently stop portaling — fail loudly
instead.

Positions the tooltip popup against its trigger (or a custom anchor).

A "thin" positioner (ADR 0012): it inherits the popper positioning surface (inputs, `placed`
output, unified vars + placement attrs) from {@link RdxPopperContentWrapper} and adds tooltip's own
concerns — Base UI-aligned defaults (`side: 'top'`) via the config provider, dismiss handling
(ADR 0015 — inline {@link RdxDismiss} on the shared floating tree, dismissal-only with
no focus manager), the cursor-follow pointer-through behavior (via the inherited `nonInteractive`
signal), the open/closed state attributes, and the hover grace area.

inputs.name: align
inputs.description: How to align the popup relative to the specified side. May change when collisions occur.

inputs.name: alignOffset
inputs.description:
An offset in pixels from the `start` or `end` alignment options. Also accepts an
{@link OffsetFunction} (same signature as `sideOffset`).

inputs.name: anchor
inputs.description: An element to position the popup against. Defaults to the popper anchor.

inputs.name: arrowPadding
inputs.description:
Minimum distance to maintain between the arrow and the edges of the popup.
If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it from overflowing the corners.

inputs.name: avoidCollisions
inputs.description:
When `true`, overrides the `side` and `align` preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

`false` disables all avoidance (unless a `collisionAvoidance` object is provided, which wins).

inputs.name: collisionAvoidance
inputs.description:
How the popup avoids colliding with the boundary edges, per axis (Base UI `collisionAvoidance`).
Overrides the deprecated `avoidCollisions` and any per-primitive preset. An object here **fully
replaces** the preset — omitted fields fall back to `side: 'flip'`, `align: 'flip'`,
`fallbackAxisSide: 'end'`, never to the preset. See {@link RdxCollisionAvoidance}.

inputs.name: collisionBoundary
inputs.description:
The element used as the collision boundary.
By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional element(s) to be included in this check.

inputs.name: collisionPadding
inputs.description:
The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur.
Accepts a number (same for all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: `{ top: 20, left: 20 }`.

inputs.name: dir
inputs.description:
Text direction, used only to resolve a logical `side` (`inline-start` / `inline-end`) to a
physical one. Falls back to an enclosing direction provider, then `ltr`.

inputs.name: hideWhenDetached
inputs.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

inputs.name: positionStrategy
inputs.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

inputs.name: side
inputs.description:
The preferred side of the anchor to render against when open.
Will be reversed when collisions occur and avoidCollisions is enabled.

Accepts the logical `'inline-start'` / `'inline-end'` in addition to the physical sides; those
resolve to `left` / `right` for the text direction (Base UI parity). The placed side reported via
`data-side` / `placedSide()` echoes the requested kind: logical in → logical out (even after a
collision flip), physical in → physical out.

inputs.name: sideOffset
inputs.description:
Distance between the anchor and the popup in pixels. Also accepts an {@link OffsetFunction} that
reads the anchor / positioner dimensions and the resolved side / align.

inputs.name: sticky
inputs.description:
The sticky behavior on the align axis. `partial` will keep the
content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at least partially
in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary
regardless.

inputs.name: updatePositionStrategy
inputs.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

outputs.name: escapeKeyDown
outputs.description: Event handler called when the escape key is down. Can be prevented.

outputs.name: pointerDownOutside
outputs.description: Event handler called when a `pointerdown` event happens outside of the popup. Can be prevented.

outputs.name: placed
outputs.description: Emits when the element is placed.

parts.description:
Shares delay configuration and an instant-open window across a group of tooltips.
Once one tooltip opens, adjacent ones open instantly until `timeout` ms after the last close.

inputs.name: closeDelay
inputs.description: How long to wait before closing tooltips in this group. Specified in milliseconds.

inputs.name: delay
inputs.description: How long to wait before opening tooltips in this group. Specified in milliseconds.

inputs.name: timeout
inputs.description: The window during which an adjacent tooltip opens instantly. Specified in milliseconds.

inputs.name: closeDelay
inputs.description: Overrides the close delay (ms) for this trigger. Falls back to the root/provider/global delay.

inputs.name: closeOnClick
inputs.description: Whether the tooltip should close when the trigger is clicked.

inputs.name: delay
inputs.description: Overrides the open delay (ms) for this trigger. Falls back to the root/provider/global delay.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: Whether this trigger is disabled. A disabled trigger never opens the tooltip.

inputs.name: handle
inputs.description: Associates this trigger with a detached tooltip root.

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: ID used to identify this trigger when opening a detached tooltip imperatively.

inputs.name: payload
inputs.description: Data associated with this trigger while it is active.

inputs.name: rdxOnPointerDown
inputs.type: (event: PointerEvent) => void | boolean | Promise<void | boolean> | undefined

inputs.name: closeDelay
inputs.description: How long to wait before closing the tooltip. Specified in milliseconds.

inputs.name: defaultOpen
inputs.description: Whether the tooltip is initially open. Uncontrolled.

inputs.name: delay
inputs.description:
How long to wait before opening the tooltip. Specified in milliseconds.
Falls back to the surrounding provider, then to the global config.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: When `true`, the tooltip will not open.

inputs.name: disableHoverablePopup
inputs.description:
When `true`, the tooltip closes as the pointer leaves the trigger instead of
staying open while the pointer moves over the popup.

inputs.name: handle
inputs.description: Associates this root with detached trigger elements.

inputs.name: open
inputs.description: Whether the tooltip is currently open.

inputs.name: trackCursorAxis
inputs.description: Determines which axis the tooltip should track the cursor on.

outputs.name: onOpenChange
outputs.description: Event handler called when the open state changes.
